[message_id: 127b687b-e49c-4d7e-aef0-868baaa6820c]

## assistant
Here’s how I’d make the homepage feel like a *real retail store* now that we have actual product data, and also remove the “call/text/whatsapp” vibe.

## 1) Replace the “generic hero” with a product-first hero
**Current:** marketing copy + schedule/call/text buttons.  
**Better retail:** one strong headline + 2 primary actions + live proof underneath.

- Headline: “Tires & wheels that fit. Delivered or installed.”
- Subhead: “Real-time inventory • competitive pricing • fast install booking”
- Primary CTAs:
  - **Shop Tires** (opens TIRES mega-menu / shop-by flow)
  - **Shop Wheels**
- Secondary: **Deals** (or “Shop by size”)

Under the CTAs, show small trust chips:
- “Price low to high sorting”
- “Run-flat / winter / all-season filters”
- “Top brands available today”

## 2) Add a “Featured products” section (real SKUs from K&M)
This instantly makes it non-generic.

Pick 8–12 items from a few “anchor” sizes that always return results (ex: 245/45R17, 275/55R20, 265/70R17, etc.) and show a grid:
- Tire image (from your asset cache if available)
- Brand + model name (displayName)
- Price (cost + $50 like PLP)
- Badges derived from your heuristics: “Run-flat”, “XL”, “Winter”
- CTA: **View in size** (links to `/tires?...&size=245/45R17`)

Even if it’s not personalized yet, it looks real.

## 3) Add “Shop by category” tiles that map to your working filters
Because you *can* power these now:
- Run-Flat Tires → `/tires?size=245/45R17&runFlat=1` (or pick a more common size)
- Winter Tires → `/tires?...&season=Winter`
- All-Season Tires → `/tires?...&season=All-season`
- Low Price Tires → `/tires?...&sort=price_asc`

These are retail-native and use functionality you already built.

## 4) Replace “conversion shell preview” with real content
That black demo panel should become either:
- **Deals & rebates** (even if “rebates” isn’t real yet, you can do “Popular picks” / “Best sellers”), or
- **Why buy from us** (shipping, install scheduling, returns), but with real store language.

## 5) Remove call/text/whatsapp blocks + make the footer feel like ecommerce
Instead of “call/text now”, use:
- Shipping/returns policy links
- Install scheduling info
- Store address + hours (if you want local retail feel)
- Payment/financing placeholders (only if true soon)

## Two quick questions so I implement the right version
1) Do you want the homepage to be **tire-first**, **wheels-first**, or **50/50**?
2) Should the primary conversion be **“Shop online”** or still **“Schedule install”** (but without phone/text buttons)?

## user
a nice 50 50 mic would be great and for the time being schedule install
[message_id: 3a51f048-5646-441b-abd6-323fd9c832c9]

## assistant
Got it: **50/50 tires + wheels**, and the primary conversion is still **Schedule Install** (just not “call/text/whatsapp” heavy).

Here’s a homepage structure that will feel like a real retail store *using the product data we already have*, while keeping scheduling as the main CTA:

## Homepage plan (retail-feeling, 50/50)

### 1) Hero: “Schedule-first”, but product-backed
Left side:
- Headline: “Tires & wheels that fit. Installed fast.”
- Subhead: “Shop real inventory, then book your install in minutes.”
- CTAs:
  - **Schedule Install** (primary)
  - **Shop Tires** and **Shop Wheels** (secondary, equal weight)

Right side:
- Replace the fake “conversion shell preview” with a **real “Today’s picks”** module:
  - 2 tire cards + 2 wheel cards (or a tab: Tires | Wheels)
  - Real price, brand, name, and “Run-flat / Winter / XL” badges when applicable.

### 2) “Shop tires” + “Shop wheels” category tiles (50/50)
A clean 2-column section:

**Shop Tires**
- All-Season
- Winter
- Run-Flat
- Budget picks (sorted low-to-high)

**Shop Wheels**
- By Vehicle
- By Size (Diameter → Width → Bolt pattern)
- Popular diameters (20”, 22”)
- “Truck/SUV wheels” (placeholder for later, but can link to /wheels)

These link into flows you already have (mega-menu + modal).

### 3) Featured products grid (real listings, split 50/50)
A “Featured right now” grid:
- 6 tires + 6 wheels
- Each card: image (if cached), brand, name, price, and a simple CTA:
  - Tires: “View tires in this size”
  - Wheels: “View wheels” (until wheel-by-size is fully wired into WheelPros)

This is the single biggest thing that makes it feel *not generic*.

### 4) Replace contact blocks with retail credibility blocks
Remove the Call/Text/WhatsApp blocks on the homepage and replace with:
- “Installation scheduling”
- “Fast local availability” (wording only for now)
- “Fitment verified at checkout” (or “before install”)

### 5) Keep a small “Need help?” link, but not a whole section
We can keep one subtle “Need help?” link in the header/footer, without making the homepage look like a lead-gen page.

---

## Next step (so I can implement cleanly)
To populate “Today’s picks” and “Featured products”, I need one decision:

**Do you want those picks to come from a fixed set of “anchor sizes”** (e.g., 245/45R17 for run-flat coverage + a truck/SUV size), or should we pick one “popular” size and keep it consistent?

If you tell me 2 tire sizes you want to feature (one car, one truck/SUV), I’ll implement the new homepage layout and wire it to real product pulls.

## user
the 2 most popular sizes are 225/65R17 and 275/60R20

## toolResult
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export const runtime = "nodejs";

export async function GET(req: Request) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const base =
    process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL ||
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL;
  if (!base) {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Missing WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL (or NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL)" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }

  const upstream = new URL("/wheels/search", base);
  url.searchParams.forEach((v, k) => upstream.searchParams.set(k, v));

  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Accept: "application/json" };
  if (process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY) {
    headers["x-api-key"] = process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY;
  }

  const res = await fetch(upstream.toString(), { headers, cache: "no-store" });
  const text = await res.text();
  return new NextResponse(text, {
    status: res.status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": res.headers.get("content-type") || "application/json",
    },
  });
}

## assistant
I updated the homepage to feel like a real retail store and removed the call/text/whatsapp-heavy stuff.

### What changed on the homepage
- **50/50 Tires + Wheels** focus (not generic marketing blocks)
- Keeps **Schedule Install** as the primary CTA
- Adds **real “Today’s picks”** using live supplier results:
  - Tires pulled from **225/65R17** and **275/60R20** (lowest-price picks)
  - Wheels pulled from WheelPros (lowest-price picks)
- Added “Shop Tires / Shop Wheels” tiles + quick category links that use filters you already have (Run-flat, Winter, low-to-high)
- Removed the old “Call/Text now” section and the generic demo PLP blocks

### Notes
- The homepage is now **dynamic** (because it fetches live product data).
- Wheels “by size” is still UI-first (as discussed), but the homepage wheel picks should still render if WheelPros returns results without fitment filters.

### Deployed
Pushed to `main`: commit **75c6341** (Vercel should auto-deploy).

If you want, next pass is making the “Today’s picks” cards show actual tire images (when cached) and adding a small “Shop {size}” strip that looks like a real retailer’s promoted sizes.

## user
.
[message_id: 032986bf-7de2-4350-9b3a-82ce79caf679]

## assistant
Because WheelPros is returning **a lot of items with `images: []`**, and our “lowest price” sort was often picking those first—so there was nothing to render.

I verified the API response from your live site (`/api/wheelpros/wheels/search…`): the first result had images, but several of the next “cheap” results had **empty images arrays**.

Fix I just deployed:
- On the homepage wheel picks, we now **prefer wheels that actually have an `imageUrl`**, then fill remaining slots with no-image items (still sorted low→high within each group).

Pushed to `main`: commit **1360e8e**.

   1360e8e..b6c8a50  main -> main

## assistant
Yes — I changed the homepage wheel picks to **only include wheels that have an imageUrl** (so no blank thumbnails).

That means you might see fewer than 6 wheel picks if WheelPros doesn’t return enough imaged items in that first page, but everything shown will have a picture.

Pushed to `main`: commit **b6c8a50** (auto-deploy on Vercel).

## toolResult
To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   b6c8a50..2f6bafc  main -> main

## assistant
Yes — and I went ahead and implemented the first step of that.

### What I changed
1) **Homepage section title**
- Changed “Wheels” → **“Featured wheels”**

2) **Clickable wheel picks (homepage)**
- Each wheel in the “Featured wheels” picks is now a link to a wheel detail URL:
  - `/wheels/[sku]`

3) **Clickable wheels on the /wheels listing page**
- Each wheel card on `/wheels` is now clickable and opens `/wheels/[sku]`

4) **New wheel product page**
- Added: `src/app/wheels/[sku]/page.tsx`
- It loads the wheel by SKU (best-effort) and shows:
  - **Large image**
  - Brand + title
  - Price
  - Basic properties (diameter/width/offset/finish) as “Options (coming next)”
  - Schedule Install button

### What’s next (your dropdowns request)
You’re right: a real wheel PDP should have dropdowns for:
- **Diameter**
- **Width**
- **Offset**
- **Finish**
(and probably bolt pattern / center bore where relevant)

To do that properly, we need to query WheelPros for “all variants of this model” (or however their API groups a product family). Right now we’re only fetching a single SKU. Next step is:
- find the best grouping key (often brand + model + finish or a “parent” id / style code if WheelPros provides one)
- fetch all matching SKUs
- build dropdown options from the variant set

### Deployed
Pushed to `main`: commit **2f6bafc** (auto-deploy on Vercel).

[message_id: 072514dd-d85c-47be-bce1-97d7901f8380]

## assistant
Done — the wheel PDP now has working dropdowns (best‑effort) for **Diameter / Width / Offset / Finish**.

### How it works (right now)
- On `/wheels/[sku]`, we:
  1) Fetch the wheel by **SKU**
  2) Derive a **model token** from the title (ex: `BR ALISO ...` → token `ALISO`)
  3) Fetch up to **200** wheels from WheelPros using:
     - `brand_cd=<brand code>` and `q=<model token>`
  4) Build dropdown option lists from the returned variants
- When you change a dropdown, it **navigates to the matching SKU** (so URL updates and image/price update).

### Notes / limitations
- This grouping is “best effort” because WheelPros doesn’t give us a clean “parent product id” in the data we’re using. We can improve it later by tightening the matching rules (ex: also require same finishCode, bolt pattern, etc.).
- If WheelPros returns a noisy result set for a token, dropdowns may include unrelated options — we’ll refine once we see a couple examples.

### Deployed
Pushed to `main`: commit **5fb9e0c**.

[main bfea6be] Wheels PDP: include properties in variant fetch; fix selector labels
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

## toolResult
To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   5fb9e0c..bfea6be  main -> main

## assistant
Right now they *look* like dropdowns, but in your build they weren’t actually able to change to a different SKU reliably because the variant fetch wasn’t returning the fields we were using to build the options.

Two fixes just shipped:

1) **Variant fetch now includes `properties`**
- I updated the WheelPros calls on the PDP to request:
  - `fields=...,properties`
So the variant list actually contains `diameter/width/offset/finish` to populate the dropdowns.

2) Cleaned up the dropdown labels (removed stray quotes on diameter/width).

After this deploy, changing a dropdown will:
- find the best matching variant SKU
- navigate to `/wheels/<that-sku>`
- which refreshes the PDP (image/title/price) accordingly.

Deployed to `main`: commit **bfea6be**.

If you open any wheel PDP and tell me one SKU you’re testing, I can verify the variant grouping is returning a meaningful set (and tighten the matching if it’s pulling unrelated SKUs).

[main 5bb61d9] Wheels PDP: cascade variant dropdowns and show part number
 2 files changed, 60 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)

## toolResult
To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   bfea6be..5bb61d9  main -> main

## assistant
Yep — that’s the right behavior, and I just updated the wheel PDP selector to work that way.

### What changed
1) **Cascading dropdowns**
- When you pick a **Diameter**, the **Width** dropdown now only shows widths that exist for that diameter.
- After Width is chosen, **Offset** only shows offsets available for that diameter+width.
- After Offset is chosen, **Finish** only shows finishes available for that diameter+width+offset.

2) **Auto-clears invalid selections**
- If you change Diameter and your previously-selected Width/Offset/Finish no longer exists, it automatically clears those so you never get “stuck” on a combo that doesn’t exist.

3) **Part number shown**
- PDP now displays: **“Part / SKU: …”**

### How it “changes something”
When you change any dropdown, it navigates to the **best matching SKU** for the selected combination:
- URL changes to `/wheels/<new-sku>`
- Page reloads with the new wheel’s image/title/price

Deployed to `main`: commit **5bb61d9**.

Next improvement (if you want it even more retail-accurate): we can disable dropdowns until the parent selection is chosen (e.g., disable Offset until Width selected), and show “X variants available” per option.

## assistant
You’re right on both points:

1) **If “Zephyr” is showing sizes up to 34"**, that means our “variant grouping” is too broad and is pulling in unrelated wheels—so the dropdowns are not specific to that exact wheel family.

2) **Bolt pattern should be an option** (and it should cascade like the rest).

I just shipped an improvement that fixes the *bolt pattern* piece and improves the cascading logic:

### What I changed (now deployed)
- Added a **Bolt pattern dropdown** to the wheel PDP.
- The dropdowns now cascade in this order:
  **Diameter → Width → Bolt pattern → Offset → Finish**
- Bolt pattern is pulled from WheelPros `properties.boltPatternMetric` (fallback to `boltPattern`).

Deployed to `main`: commit **94c0c08**.

---

### What’s still needed (to fix the “34 inch” issue)
The core issue is the **grouping key**. Right now we group variants by:
- `brand_cd` + a “model token” derived from the title (2nd word)

For some wheels (like Zephyr) that token is too generic and matches a bunch of unrelated SKUs, so you get bogus diameters/widths/offsets/finishes.

**Next fix (recommended): tighten variant grouping**
We should filter the variant set to only those that match *the same wheel family*. Best ways, in order:

A) If WheelPros returns a true family/model id (sometimes `model` or a style code), use that.  
B) If not, use a strict match on:
- same `brand_cd`
- same “model token”
- and also require at least one of:
  - same `finishCode` prefix, or
  - same `title` starts with the same “brand + model” string, or
  - same `images[0].imageUrlLarge` base path (often shared within a wheel family)

---

### Quick ask
Can you paste the **exact SKU** of the “Zephyr” wheel you’re on (`Part / SKU:` on the PDP), or just the URL `/wheels/<sku>`?

I’ll use that one SKU to tune the grouping so the Diameter list only shows the real sizes for that specific wheel, then roll it out across the PDP.

## user
i opened a search results page in wheel pros for the exact wheel that gives sku sizes offset and finishes. does that help
[message_id: c0504f78-2ffe-48e3-bc93-b9d3e4e5b716]

## assistant
Yes — that helps a lot.

If WheelPros can show a results page that already has the **exact variant set** (SKUs + diameter/width + offset + finish + bolt pattern), then we can stop guessing and make our PDP dropdowns match that same dataset.

### What I need from that WheelPros page
Either:

1) **The URL** of the WheelPros results page you’re looking at (best), or  
2) The **SKU** you searched + the **filters** you applied (brand/model/finish), or  
3) If it’s behind login and the URL won’t work, tell me the **tab title** and I can try to grab the data from the managed browser (or you can copy/paste one example row list).

### Why this matters
Right now our grouping is “brand + token”, which can pull unrelated wheels. If we can replicate the exact WheelPros query that returns only that wheel family, then the dropdowns will be correct and will cascade properly.

Send me the URL (or SKU), and I’ll:
- reproduce the exact query in our `/api/wheelpros/wheels/search`
- use that response as the variant list for the PDP
- ensure dropdowns narrow like DiscountTire: pick diameter → only valid widths → only valid bolt patterns → offsets → finishes.

[main a2a558c] Wheels PDP: tighten variant grouping using title prefix; better model token parsing

## user
.
[message_id: 00dc534f-967b-41f8-aa1d-8301c7415fe4]

## toolResult
To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   94c0c08..a2a558c  main -> main

## assistant
Yes, that WheelPros “Search Results” tab helps a ton — and I was able to find it in the managed browser:

`https://dl.wheelpros.com/us_en/ymm/search/?...&q=ZEPHYR...`

That confirms the right approach: our PDP needs to use a **much tighter variant set**, not “everything that matches a loose token”, otherwise you’ll see crazy sizes like 34".

### What I changed (to fix the “34 inch sizes” problem)
I tightened the variant grouping logic in the PDP:

1) **Better model token parsing**
- Instead of taking only the 2nd word, we now extract the model name up until the first size token like `18X9`.

2) **Hard narrowing by title prefix**
- After we fetch variants using `brand_cd + q=modelToken`, we now filter to only variants whose title starts with:
  - `"<BRANDCODE> <MODELTOKEN>"`
Example: `BR ZEPHYR ...`  
This drastically reduces unrelated matches and should stop the bogus diameter list.

### Bolt pattern
We already added the **bolt pattern dropdown** and it cascades now:
**Diameter → Width → Bolt pattern → Offset → Finish**

### Deployed
Pushed to `main`: commit **a2a558c**.

If you refresh the Zephyr PDP now, the diameter list should be limited to the real Zephyr variants (not all the way to 34"). If it’s still too broad, send me the exact SKU you were on (from “Part / SKU”) and I’ll tighten it further (next step would be matching also on finishCode/width patterns or pulling variants from WheelPros facets instead of free-text search).

## user
now will this also roll over ot all other wheel and have the same properties
[message_id: 8dc48e2b-1dc0-4aa9-be11-d46e19245201]

## assistant
Yes — this change applies to **all wheel PDPs** (`/wheels/[sku]`), not just Zephyr.

What will “roll over” everywhere:

- **Variant grouping is tighter** for every wheel:
  - we derive a model token from the title
  - we fetch candidates with `brand_cd + q=<model token>`
  - then we **narrow** to only titles that start with `"<brand code> <model token>"`

- **Same dropdown behavior** on every wheel PDP:
  - cascades: **Diameter → Width → Bolt pattern → Offset → Finish**
  - changing a dropdown navigates to the best matching **SKU**
  - shows **Part / SKU** on the page

Caveat (honest one):
- This is still “best-effort” because WheelPros doesn’t give us a clean universal “parent product id” in the data we’re pulling. So on some wheel names that are ambiguous, the variant list might still be imperfect—but it should be *much* better than before, and consistent across all wheels.

If you tell me 1–2 more wheel names/SKUs that look wrong after the deploy, I can refine the grouping rules to handle those patterns too.

i refreshed and now the drop does not change sizes at all

[main 131e70f] Wheels PDP: parse missing variant fields from title; avoid grouping when no model token
 1 file changed, 25 insertions(+), 9 deletions(-)

## toolResult
To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   a2a558c..131e70f  main -> main

## assistant
What happened on that wheel is the key clue:

That SKU (`F22218806140`) is returning **blank properties** from WheelPros (bolt pattern empty, finish empty, images empty). So our dropdowns were getting built from the *broader variant fetch* (200 items), which makes sizes look wrong and also makes it feel like changing selections “does nothing” because it’s not actually a real variant family.

I just fixed two things to make this behave correctly:

### 1) Don’t “variant-group” when there’s no real model name
Some WheelPros titles start with size like:
`18X8 P33 5X120.7 ...`
That’s not a wheel name like “ZEPHYR”, so our previous “model token” extraction was basically garbage and caused the huge unrelated variant set.

Now:
- we only attempt variant grouping if the extracted model token contains letters (a real name)
- otherwise we fall back to a single-variant selector (no bogus size list)

### 2) Parse missing data from the title when WheelPros leaves properties blank
When `properties.boltPatternMetric/boltPattern` are blank (like your example), we now parse from `title`:
- diameter/width from `18X8`
- bolt pattern from `5X120.7`
- offset from `12MM` / `38MM` patterns (when present)

That means the dropdowns will have real values even when WP properties are empty.

Deployed to `main`: commit **131e70f**.

For “ZEPHYR” specifically: it should now only show the real Zephyr variant diameters/widths, and bolt pattern should populate if it’s present in the title or properties. If you tell me the Zephyr SKU you were on, I can verify the grouping result set is tight and matches the WheelPros “Search Results” page variants.

## assistant
Yes — this helps *a lot*. That Wheel_TechGuide.csv has exactly the fields we’re missing/guessing from the live API:

- diameter / width / offset / backspacing / centerbore  
- lug_count + bolt_pattern_metric/standard  
- brand/style identifiers (`brand_cd`, `style`, `display_style_no`)  
- **image_url** (and image_url1–4)  
- MSRP/MAP, weights, warranties, etc.

This gives us a clean way to build a **proper wheel PDP variant matrix** (and later, wheel search facets) without relying on fuzzy title parsing or the WheelPros API returning incomplete `properties`.

### What we can do next (recommended)
1) **Import the CSV into a local

## user
.
[message_id: d43e37c8-284d-47a3-ad12-d0aa27977b78]

## assistant
Yes—this is basically the missing piece.

Right now the live WheelPros API sometimes gives us incomplete `properties` (you literally had blank bolt pattern/finish/images). The **TechGuide CSV has the canonical, complete fitment + variant data**, so we can:

- build dropdown options from *real* fields (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset/finish)
- group variants correctly using stable identifiers like **`style`** / **`display_style_no`** (instead of guessing from title)
- show correct images consistently (`image_url1..4`)

### Best way to use it
**Step 1: Ingest `Wheel_TechGuide.csv` into a fast lookup store**
Options:
- **SQLite** (best for size/perf; 29MB CSV is fine, but parsing every request isn’t)
- or a **JSON/NDJSON index** by `sku` + by `style` (simpler, still fast)

**Step 2: Add an internal API**
- `/api/techfeed/wheels/by-sku?sku=...`
- `/api/techfeed/wheels/by-style?style=...` (or by `display_style_no`)

**Step 3: Update the wheel PDP**
- Fetch techfeed row for current SKU → get its `style` → fetch all rows for that style → build the variant selector from that set.
This will make the dropdowns actually change sizes/offsets/finishes reliably.

### Two quick questions so I implement it the way you want
1) Is it OK to treat the **techfeed as the source of truth** for wheel variant attributes (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset/finish/images), and only fall back to live WheelPros API if SKU isn’t found?
2) Do you want this to run in production on Vercel?  
   - If yes, we’ll need to store the processed index somewhere Vercel can access (e.g., committed JSON index, Vercel Blob, S3, or a small DB). Reading raw CSV from the repo at runtime is possible but not ideal.

If you say “yes, source of truth”, I’ll start by building a SQLite index + API routes locally, then we can decide the best hosting/storage for deployment.

[message_id: 789ba17d-9137-4238-b07b-9664fbf0f12f]

## assistant
I need one decision from you before I wire this in:

1) **Source of truth:** Should the TechFeed CSV be the primary data for wheel attributes (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset/finish/images), with WheelPros API only as a fallback if a SKU isn’t in the feed?

2) **Where should the indexed data live for production (Vercel)?** Pick one:
- **A)** Commit a generated index file into the repo (simple, but increases repo size)
- **B)** Put it in a hosted store (Vercel Blob / S3) and download at runtime/startup (cleaner)
- **C)** Use a small DB (SQLite won’t work well on Vercel serverless unless it’s hosted; Postgres/etc if you already have one)

If you tell me “1=yes” and pick A/B/C, I’ll implement:
- an ingest script that turns `Wheel_TechGuide.csv` into an index (keyed by `sku` and `style`)
- API routes to query it
- PDP variant selector powered by `style` grouping (no more fuzzy title guessing).

## user
where would you it? and the file also shows image url so we can populate all all images now
[message_id: bb28acd9-43ee-4b55-89f0-6a9e839288f0]

## assistant
I’d store it **outside the repo** and treat it like a data artifact, not source code.

### My recommendation (best fit for Vercel)
**Use Vercel Blob (or S3 if you already have it)** and upload a *processed* index derived from the CSV.

Why:
- The wheel CSV is big and changes weekly. Committing it (or a huge derived JSON) will bloat the repo and slow deploys.
- Vercel serverless instances have ephemeral disk; you *can* read files committed in the repo at runtime, but it’s not great for large, frequently-updated datasets.
- Blob/S3 lets us update the dataset without a code deploy (or at least keeps deploys light).

### What we store there (not the raw CSV)
Instead of loading/parsing a 29MB CSV at runtime, we generate one or two compact indexes, e.g.:

- `wheels_by_sku.json` (or `.json.gz`):
  - key: sku
  - value: the fields we need on PDP/search (diameter, width, bolt_pattern_metric, offset, finish desc, style, images, etc.)

- `skus_by_style.json`:
  - key: style (or display_style_no)
  - value: array of SKUs (or minimal variant rows)

Then on the PDP:
1) lookup SKU → get its `style`
2) get all variants for that `style`
3) build dropdowns from that set (no fuzzy title grouping)

### Images: yes, we should use them
The techfeed’s `image_url`, `image_url1..4` means we can finally populate:
- main hero image + alt images reliably
- (and stop showing “No image” when WheelPros API returns empty `images`)

### One question before I implement
Do you want weekly updates to be **manual** (you drop the CSV in `techfeed/` and I run a script) or **automatic** (cron job to ingest the latest CSV + upload the new indexes to Blob/S3)?

If you say “manual is fine for now”, I’ll build:
- `scripts/techfeed-build.ts` → reads CSV → outputs the two index files (gzipped)
- an API helper that loads/caches the index from Blob/S3
- update the wheel PDP variant selector + images to use techfeed first, WheelPros API fallback.

---
summary: "Cron jobs + wakeups for the Gateway scheduler"
read_when:
  - Scheduling background jobs or wakeups
  - Wiring automation that should run with or alongside heartbeats
  - Deciding between heartbeat and cron for scheduled tasks
---
# Cron jobs (Gateway scheduler)

> **Cron vs Heartbeat?** See [Cron vs Heartbeat](/automation/cron-vs-heartbeat) for guidance on when to use each.

Cron is the Gateway’s built-in scheduler. It persists jobs, wakes the agent at
the right time, and can optionally deliver output back to a chat.

If you want *“run this every morning”* or *“poke the agent in 20 minutes”*,
cron is the mechanism.

## TL;DR
- Cron runs **inside the Gateway** (not inside the model).
- Jobs persist under `~/.clawdbot/cron/` so restarts don’t lose schedules.
- Two execution styles:
  - **Main session**: enqueue a system event, then run on the next heartbeat.
  - **Isolated**: run a dedicated agent turn in `cron:<jobId>`, optionally deliver output.
- Wakeups are first-class: a job can request “wake now” vs “next heartbeat”.

## Beginner-friendly overview
Think of a cron job as: **when** to run + **what** to do.

1) **Choose a schedule**
   - One-shot reminder → `schedule.kind = "at"` (CLI: `--at`)
   - Repeating job → `schedule.kind = "every"` or `schedule.kind = "cron"`
   - If your ISO timestamp omits a timezone, it is treated as **UTC**.

2) **Choose where it runs**
   - `sessionTarget: "main"` → run during the next heartbeat with main context.
   - `sessionTarget: "isolated"` → run a dedicated agent turn in `cron:<jobId>`.

3) **Choose the payload**
   - Main session → `payload.kind = "systemEvent"`
   - Isolated session → `payload.kind = "agentTurn"`

Optional: `deleteAfterRun: true` removes successful one-shot jobs from the store.

## Concepts

### Jobs
A cron job is a stored record with:
- a **schedule** (when it should run),
- a **payload** (what it should do),
- optional **delivery** (where output should be sent).
- optional **agent binding** (`agentId`): run the job under a specific agent; if
  missing or unknown, the gateway falls back to the default agent.

Jobs are identified by a stable `jobId` (used by CLI/Gateway APIs).
In agent tool calls, `jobId` is canonical; legacy `id` is accepted for compatibility.
Jobs can optionally auto-delete after a successful one-shot run via `deleteAfterRun: true`.

### Schedules
Cron supports three schedule kinds:
- `at`: one-shot timestamp (ms since epoch). Gateway accepts ISO 8601 and coerces to UTC.
- `every`: fixed interval (ms).
- `cron`: 5-field cron expression with optional IANA timezone.

Cron expressions use `croner`. If a timezone is omitted, the Gateway host’s
local timezone is used.

### Main vs isolated execution

#### Main session jobs (system events)
Main jobs enqueue a system event and optionally wake the heartbeat runner.
They must use `payload.kind = "systemEvent"`.

- `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"` (default): event waits for the next scheduled heartbeat.
- `wakeMode: "now"`: event triggers an immediate heartbeat run.

This is the best fit when you want the normal heartbeat prompt + main-session context.
See [Heartbeat](/gateway/heartbeat).

#### Isolated jobs (dedicated cron sessions)
Isolated jobs run a dedicated agent turn in session `cron:<jobId>`.

Key behaviors:
- Prompt is prefixed with `[cron:<jobId> <job name>]` for traceability.
- Each run starts a **fresh session id** (no prior conversation carry-over).
- A summary is posted to the main session (prefix `Cron`, configurable).
- `wakeMode: "now"` triggers an immediate heartbeat after posting the summary.
- If `payload.deliver: true`, output is delivered to a channel; otherwise it stays internal.

Use isolated jobs for noisy, frequent, or "background chores" that shouldn't spam
your main chat history.

### Payload shapes (what runs)
Two payload kinds are supported:
- `systemEvent`: main-session only, routed through the heartbeat prompt.
- `agentTurn`: isolated-session only, runs a dedicated agent turn.

Common `agentTurn` fields:
- `message`: required text prompt.
- `model` / `thinking`: optional overrides (see below).
- `timeoutSeconds`: optional timeout override.
- `deliver`: `true` to send output to a channel target.
- `channel`: `last` or a specific channel.
- `to`: channel-specific target (phone/chat/channel id).
- `bestEffortDeliver`: avoid failing the job if delivery fails.

Isolation options (only for `session=isolated`):
- `postToMainPrefix` (CLI: `--post-prefix`): prefix for the system event in main.
- `postToMainMode`: `summary` (default) or `full`.
- `postToMainMaxChars`: max chars when `postToMainMode=full` (default 8000).

### Model and thinking overrides
Isolated jobs (`agentTurn`) can override the model and thinking level:
- `model`: Provider/model string (e.g., `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) or alias (e.g., `opus`)
- `thinking`: Thinking level (`off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`; GPT-5.2 + Codex models only)

Note: You can set `model` on main-session jobs too, but it changes the shared main
session model. We recommend model overrides only for isolated jobs to avoid
unexpected context shifts.

## assistant
Yep—massively.

That TechFeed wheel CSV has the *complete* canonical variant attributes (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset/finish/etc) **and** `image_url1..4`, which means we can:

- build the wheel PDP dropdowns from accurate data (no more guessing / blank properties)
- group variants correctly by a stable key like **`style`** / **`display_style_no`**
- reliably populate **all wheel images** (and stop getting “No image”)

### Plan (manual for now)
- You drop the new weekly CSVs into `clawd/techfeed/`
- We run an ingest step that generates a compact index (by `sku`, and by `style`)
- The app uses that index first; live WheelPros API only as fallback

### Reminder set
I set a one-shot reminder for **Fri Mar 20, 2026 @ 10:00 AM (ET)** to revisit making this automatic (download → ingest → publish).

When you’re ready, I’ll start implementing the ingest/index + updating the wheel PDP to use TechFeed for variants + images.

## user
we can also do this for the tires and to apply msrp pricing from the csv file right
[message_id: 9c6ddf02-b0a4-4068-a3c9-a5e800904421]

## assistant
Yes—same idea applies to **tires** too, and it’s a good way to apply **MSRP/MAP** consistently.

What we can do with `Tire_TechGuide.csv` (and `MapPrice_TechGuide.csv` if that’s the authoritative MAP feed):

### Tires: what we can power from the CSV
- **MSRP / MAP price** per SKU (and keep it stable even if an API response is missing it)
- Tire attributes that help search/filters:
  - size, load index, speed rating, season, run-flat, treadwear, etc. (depending on columns in that file)
- Better images/specs if the CSV includes them

### How pricing would work (recommended)
- Use the CSV as the **base pricing source**:
  - `msrp` from Tire_TechGuide
  - `map_price` from MapPrice_TechGuide (if present, override MSRP display rules)
- Keep your current “live availability / inventory” lookup as-is (that’s usually not in the static feed)

### Implementation approach (same pattern as wheels)
- Build an index keyed by **tire SKU**:
  - `{ sku -> { msrp, map, key attributes } }`
- In your tire search + PDP API responses:
  - merge in techfeed MSRP/MAP fields
  - fall back to existing provider/API price if the SKU isn’t found

### One quick check before I code it
Do you want **MSRP/MAP to override whatever TireConnect/KM returns**, or only fill in blanks?

If you say “override”, we’ll always display techfeed MSRP/MAP when present (clean + consistent). If you say “fill blanks”, we only use it when the live data doesn’t provide pricing.

Got it.

- **Wheels (WheelPros):** we’ll use TechFeed to supply **MSRP** (and MAP if you later want it), plus the complete variant attributes + images.
- **Tires:** leave pricing logic alone and keep your **+$50 markup** exactly as it is now. We can still optionally use the tire CSV later for *attributes/images/search faceting*, but **not for price**.

If you want, I’ll bake this rule in explicitly as:
- `techfeed pricing applies only when skuType === WHEEL (WheelPros)` (or when the wheel page/data source is WheelPros)
- tire routes never read MSRP from techfeed for pricing.

import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export const runtime = "nodejs";

export async function GET(req: Request) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const base =
    process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL ||
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL;
  if (!base) {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Missing WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL (or NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL)" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }

  const upstream = new URL("/wheels/search", base);
  url.searchParams.forEach((v, k) => upstream.searchParams.set(k, v));

  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Accept: "application/json" };
  if (process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY) {
    headers["x-api-key"] = process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY;
  }

  const res = await fetch(upstream.toString(), { headers, cache: "no-store" });
  const text = await res.text();
  return new NextResponse(text, {
    status: res.status,
    headers: {
      "content-type": res.headers.get("content-type") || "application/json",
    },
  });
}

## toolResult
import Link from "next/link";
import { WheelVariantSelector, type WheelVariant } from "@/components/WheelVariantSelector";

type WheelProsBrand = {
  code?: string;
  description?: string;
  parent?: string;
};

type WheelProsImage = {
  imageUrlOriginal?: string;
  imageUrlSmall?: string;
  imageUrlMedium?: string;
  imageUrlLarge?: string;
};

type WheelProsPrice = {
  currencyAmount?: string;
  currencyCode?: string;
};

type WheelProsItem = {
  sku?: string;
  title?: string;
  brand?: WheelProsBrand | string;
  properties?: {
    model?: string;
    finish?: string;
    width?: string;
    diameter?: string;
    offset?: string;
    boltPattern?: string;
    boltPatternMetric?: string;
    centerbore?: string;
  };
  prices?: {
    msrp?: WheelProsPrice[];
  };
  images?: WheelProsImage[];
};

function getBaseUrl() {
  if (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL) return process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL;
  if (process.env.VERCEL_URL) return `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}`;
  return "http://localhost:3000";
}

async function fetchWheelBySku(sku: string) {
  // Best-effort: WheelPros search supports sku as a filter in our wrapper.
  const res = await fetch(
    `${getBaseUrl()}/api/wheelpros/wheels/search?fields=inventory,price,images,properties&priceType=msrp&currencyCode=USD&page=1&pageSize=1&sku=${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`,
    { cache: "no-store" }
  );
  if (!res.ok) return { error: await res.text() };
  return res.json();
}

function extractModelToken(title: string) {
  // Titles often look like:
  // "BR ALISO 18X9 6X5.5 G-BRONZE 12MM"
  // But some titles begin with size: "18X8 P33 5X120.7 ..."
  // We only want a model token when it's clearly a name (letters), otherwise return "".
  const parts = String(title || "").trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean);
  if (parts.length < 2) return "";

  const rest = parts.slice(1);
  const sizeIdx = rest.findIndex((p) => /^\d+(\.\d+)?X\d+(\.\d+)?$/i.test(p));
  const modelParts = sizeIdx >= 0 ? rest.slice(0, sizeIdx) : rest.slice(0, 2);
  const token = modelParts.join(" ").trim();

  // If the token doesn't contain any letters, it's not a model name.
  if (!/[A-Z]/i.test(token)) return "";
  return token;
}

function parseVariant(it: WheelProsItem): WheelVariant | null {
  const sku = it?.sku ? String(it.sku) : "";
  if (!sku) return null;

  const p = it?.properties || {};
  const title = String(it?.title || "");

  // Fallback parsing from title when WheelPros leaves properties blank.
  const sizeMatch = title.toUpperCase().match(/\b(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)X(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\b/);
  const diameterFromTitle = sizeMatch ? sizeMatch[1] : undefined;
  const widthFromTitle = sizeMatch ? sizeMatch[2] : undefined;

  const boltMatch = title.toUpperCase().match(/\b(\d{4,5}|\d)X(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\b/);
  const boltFromTitle = boltMatch ? `${boltMatch[1]}X${boltMatch[2]}` : undefined;

  const offsetMatch = title.toUpperCase().match(/\b(-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*MM\b/);
  const offsetFromTitle = offsetMatch ? offsetMatch[1] : undefined;

  const diameter = p?.diameter != null ? String(p.diameter) : diameterFromTitle;
  const width = p?.width != null ? String(p.width) : widthFromTitle;
  const offset = p?.offset != null ? String(p.offset) : offsetFromTitle;
  const finish = p?.finish != null ? String(p.finish) : undefined;
  const boltPattern =
    p?.boltPatternMetric != null
      ? String(p.boltPatternMetric)
      : (p?.boltPattern != null ? String(p.boltPattern) : boltFromTitle);

  return { sku, diameter, width, boltPattern, offset, finish };
}

async function fetchVariants({
  brandCode,
  modelToken,
}: {
  brandCode?: string;
  modelToken?: string;
}) {
  // Only attempt variant grouping when we have a clear model token.
  if (!brandCode || !modelToken || modelToken.length < 2) return [] as WheelVariant[];

  const res = await fetch(
    `${getBaseUrl()}/api/wheelpros/wheels/search?fields=inventory,price,images,properties&page=1&pageSize=200&brand_cd=${encodeURIComponent(brandCode)}&q=${encodeURIComponent(modelToken)}`,
    { cache: "no-store" }
  );
  if (!res.ok) return [] as WheelVariant[];

  const data = (await res.json()) as { items?: unknown[]; results?: unknown[] };
  const raw: unknown[] = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results : [];

  const parsed = raw
    .map((u) => ({ it: u as WheelProsItem, v: parseVariant(u as WheelProsItem) }))
    .filter((x): x is { it: WheelProsItem; v: WheelVariant } => !!x.v);

  const prefix = `${String(brandCode).toUpperCase()} ${String(modelToken).toUpperCase()}`.trim();
  const narrowed = parsed
    .filter(({ it }) => {
      const t = String(it?.title || "").toUpperCase();
      return t.startsWith(prefix);
    })
    .map(({ v }) => v);

  return narrowed.length ? narrowed : parsed.map(({ v }) => v);
}

export default async function WheelDetailPage({
  params,
}: {
  params: Promise<{ sku: string }>;
}) {
  const { sku } = await params;
  const data = await fetchWheelBySku(sku);

  const maybeData = data as { items?: unknown[]; results?: unknown[]; error?: unknown };
  const rawItems: unknown[] = Array.isArray(maybeData?.items)
    ? maybeData.items
    : (Array.isArray(maybeData?.results) ? maybeData.results : []);

  const it = (rawItems[0] as WheelProsItem | undefined) || undefined;

  if (maybeData?.error || !it) {
    return (
      <main className="bg-neutral-50">
        <div className="mx-auto max-w-6xl px-4 py-10">
          <div className="rounded-2xl border border-red-200 bg-red-50 p-4 text-sm text-red-900">
            Wheel not found (SKU: {sku}).
            <div className="mt-2 text-xs text-red-800">
              {maybeData?.error ? String(maybeData.error).slice(0, 500) : ""}
            </div>
          </div>
          <div className="mt-4">
            <Link href="/wheels" className="text-sm font-extrabold text-neutral-900 hover:underline">
              ← Back to wheels
            </Link>
          </div>
        </div>
      </main>
    );
  }

  const brandObj = it?.brand && typeof it.brand === "object" ? (it.brand as WheelProsBrand) : null;
  const brand = brandObj?.description ?? brandObj?.parent ?? brandObj?.code ?? (typeof it?.brand === "string" ? it.brand : undefined);

  const msrp = it?.prices?.msrp;
  const firstPrice = Array.isArray(msrp) ? msrp[0] : undefined;
  const price = firstPrice?.currencyAmount != null ? Number(firstPrice.currencyAmount) : undefined;

  const img0 = Array.isArray(it?.images) ? it.images[0] : undefined;
  const imageUrl = img0?.imageUrlLarge || img0?.imageUrlMedium || img0?.imageUrlOriginal || undefined;

  const diameter = it?.properties?.diameter != null ? String(it.properties.diameter) : "";
  const width = it?.properties?.width != null ? String(it.properties.width) : "";
  const boltPattern = it?.properties?.boltPatternMetric != null
    ? String(it.properties.boltPatternMetric)
    : (it?.properties?.boltPattern != null ? String(it.properties.boltPattern) : "");
  const offset = it?.properties?.offset != null ? String(it.properties.offset) : "";
  const finish = it?.properties?.finish != null ? String(it.properties.finish) : "";

  const brandCode = brandObj?.code || (typeof it?.brand === "object" ? (it.brand as WheelProsBrand | undefined)?.code : undefined);
  const modelToken = extractModelToken(String(it?.title || ""));
  const variants = await fetchVariants({ brandCode, modelToken });
  const variantsForSelector = variants.length
    ? variants
    : ([{ sku, diameter, width, boltPattern, offset, finish }].filter((v) => v.sku) as WheelVariant[]);

  return (
    <main className="bg-neutral-50">
      <div className="mx-auto max-w-6xl px-4 py-10">
        <div className="flex items-center justify-between gap-3">
          <Link href="/wheels" className="text-sm font-extrabold text-neutral-900 hover:underline">
            ← Back to wheels
          </Link>
        </div>

        <div className="mt-6 grid gap-6 md:grid-cols-2">
          <div className="rounded-3xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white p-4">
            <div className="overflow-hidden rounded-2xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white">
              {imageUrl ? (
                // eslint-disable-next-line @next/next/no-img-element
                <img
                  src={imageUrl}
                  alt={it?.title || sku}
                  className="h-[360px] w-full object-contain"
                  loading="lazy"
                />
              ) : (

## assistant
Yep — let’s apply it now. I just wired your **Wheel_TechGuide.csv** into the site (manual workflow) and pushed it to `main`.

### What’s now happening
**For WheelPros wheels only:**
- **MSRP** now comes from the TechFeed CSV (when a SKU exists in the feed)
- **Images** now come from the TechFeed (`image_url1..4`) when WheelPros API returns no images
- **Variant dropdowns** (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset/finish) now come from TechFeed and are grouped by the stable **`style`** (fallback to `display_style_no`) — so changing a dropdown should actually navigate to the correct SKU

**Tires are untouched** (your +$50 markup stays exactly as-is).

### Manual update workflow (what you’ll do weekly)
From the repo folder:
1) Drop the new CSV at: `clawd/techfeed/Wheel_TechGuide.csv` (same as you did)
2) Run:
   - `npm run techfeed:wheels`
3) Commit + push the regenerated files in `src/techfeed/*.json.gz`

### Code shipped
Commit: **e7f4e9a**  
Includes:
- `npm run techfeed:wheels` build script
- `src/techfeed/wheels_by_sku.json.gz` + `wheels_by_style.json.gz`
- API merge in `/api/wheelpros/wheels/search` (MSRP + images + missing properties)
- PDP uses TechFeed style-grouped variants first

If you refresh a wheel PDP now, the dropdowns should stop being “stuck” and the image should populate for wheels that previously showed “No image”. If you tell me one SKU that still misbehaves, I’ll trace whether it’s missing from the CSV or has a weird style key.

## toolResult
"use client";

import { useMemo } from "react";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";

export type WheelVariant = {
  sku: string;
  diameter?: string;
  width?: string;
  boltPattern?: string;
  offset?: string;
  finish?: string;
};

function uniqSorted(values: string[]) {
  return Array.from(new Set(values.filter(Boolean))).sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b, undefined, { numeric: true }));
}

export function WheelVariantSelector({
  variants,
  currentSku,
  selected,
}: {
  variants: WheelVariant[];
  currentSku: string;
  selected: { diameter?: string; width?: string; boltPattern?: string; offset?: string; finish?: string };
}) {
  const router = useRouter();

  const options = useMemo(() => {
    const diameters = uniqSorted(variants.map((v) => v.diameter || ""));

    const byDiameter = selected.diameter
      ? variants.filter((v) => v.diameter === selected.diameter)
      : variants;

    const widths = uniqSorted(byDiameter.map((v) => v.width || ""));

    const byDiameterWidth = selected.width
      ? byDiameter.filter((v) => v.width === selected.width)
      : byDiameter;

    const boltPatterns = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidth.map((v) => v.boltPattern || ""));

    const byDiameterWidthBolt = selected.boltPattern
      ? byDiameterWidth.filter((v) => v.boltPattern === selected.boltPattern)
      : byDiameterWidth;

    const offsets = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidthBolt.map((v) => v.offset || ""));

    const byDiameterWidthBoltOffset = selected.offset
      ? byDiameterWidthBolt.filter((v) => v.offset === selected.offset)
      : byDiameterWidthBolt;

    const finishes = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidthBoltOffset.map((v) => v.finish || ""));

    return { diameters, widths, boltPatterns, offsets, finishes };
  }, [variants, selected.diameter, selected.width, selected.boltPattern, selected.offset]);

  function pickSku(nextSel: { diameter?: string; width?: string; boltPattern?: string; offset?: string; finish?: string }) {
    const exact = variants.find(
      (v) =>
        (!nextSel.diameter || v.diameter === nextSel.diameter) &&
        (!nextSel.width || v.width === nextSel.width) &&
        (!nextSel.offset || v.offset === nextSel.offset) &&
        (!nextSel.finish || v.finish === nextSel.finish)
    );
    if (exact?.sku) return exact.sku;

    // Fallback: try to keep the most specific filters first
    const soft = variants.find(
      (v) =>
        (!nextSel.diameter || v.diameter === nextSel.diameter) &&
        (!nextSel.width || v.width === nextSel.width) &&
        (!nextSel.boltPattern || v.boltPattern === nextSel.boltPattern) &&
        (!nextSel.offset || v.offset === nextSel.offset)
    );
    return soft?.sku || currentSku;
  }

  function coerce(nextSel: { diameter?: string; width?: string; boltPattern?: string; offset?: string; finish?: string }) {
    // If a dependent selection no longer exists under the current constraints, clear it.
    const byDiameter = nextSel.diameter ? variants.filter((v) => v.diameter === nextSel.diameter) : variants;
    const widths = uniqSorted(byDiameter.map((v) => v.width || ""));
    if (nextSel.width && !widths.includes(nextSel.width)) nextSel.width = undefined;

    const byDiameterWidth = nextSel.width ? byDiameter.filter((v) => v.width === nextSel.width) : byDiameter;
    const boltPatterns = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidth.map((v) => v.boltPattern || ""));
    if (nextSel.boltPattern && !boltPatterns.includes(nextSel.boltPattern)) nextSel.boltPattern = undefined;

    const byDiameterWidthBolt = nextSel.boltPattern
      ? byDiameterWidth.filter((v) => v.boltPattern === nextSel.boltPattern)
      : byDiameterWidth;

    const offsets = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidthBolt.map((v) => v.offset || ""));
    if (nextSel.offset && !offsets.includes(nextSel.offset)) nextSel.offset = undefined;

    const byDiameterWidthBoltOffset = nextSel.offset
      ? byDiameterWidthBolt.filter((v) => v.offset === nextSel.offset)
      : byDiameterWidthBolt;
    const finishes = uniqSorted(byDiameterWidthBoltOffset.map((v) => v.finish || ""));
    if (nextSel.finish && !finishes.includes(nextSel.finish)) nextSel.finish = undefined;

    return nextSel;
  }

  return (
    <div className="grid gap-3">
      <div className="grid gap-2 sm:grid-cols-2">
        <label className="grid gap-1">
          <span className="text-xs font-extrabold text-neutral-900">Diameter</span>
          <select
            value={selected.diameter || ""}
            onChange={(e) => {
              const next = coerce({
                ...selected,
                diameter: e.currentTarget.value || undefined,
              });
              const sku = pickSku(next);
              router.push(`/wheels/${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`);
            }}
            className="h-11 rounded-xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold"
          >
            {options.diameters.map((d) => (
              <option key={d} value={d}>
                {d}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>

        <label className="grid gap-1">
          <span className="text-xs font-extrabold text-neutral-900">Width</span>
          <select
            value={selected.width || ""}
            onChange={(e) => {
              const next = coerce({
                ...selected,
                width: e.currentTarget.value || undefined,
              });
              const sku = pickSku(next);
              router.push(`/wheels/${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`);
            }}
            className="h-11 rounded-xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold"
          >
            {options.widths.map((w) => (
              <option key={w} value={w}>
                {w}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>

        <label className="grid gap-1">
          <span className="text-xs font-extrabold text-neutral-900">Bolt pattern</span>
          <select
            value={selected.boltPattern || ""}
            onChange={(e) => {
              const next = coerce({
                ...selected,
                boltPattern: e.currentTarget.value || undefined,
              });
              const sku = pickSku(next);
              router.push(`/wheels/${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`);
            }}
            className="h-11 rounded-xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold"
          >
            {options.boltPatterns.map((bp) => (
              <option key={bp} value={bp}>
                {bp}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>

        <label className="grid gap-1">
          <span className="text-xs font-extrabold text-neutral-900">Offset</span>
          <select
            value={selected.offset || ""}
            onChange={(e) => {
              const next = coerce({
                ...selected,
                offset: e.currentTarget.value || undefined,
              });
              const sku = pickSku(next);
              router.push(`/wheels/${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`);
            }}
            className="h-11 rounded-xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold"
          >
            {options.offsets.map((o) => (
              <option key={o} value={o}>
                {o}mm
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>

        <label className="grid gap-1">
          <span className="text-xs font-extrabold text-neutral-900">Finish</span>
          <select
            value={selected.finish || ""}
            onChange={(e) => {
              const next = coerce({
                ...selected,
                finish: e.currentTarget.value || undefined,
              });
              const sku = pickSku(next);
              router.push(`/wheels/${encodeURIComponent(sku)}`);
            }}
            className="h-11 rounded-xl border border-neutral-200 bg-white px-3 text-sm font-semibold"
          >
            {options.finishes.map((f) => (
              <option key={f} value={f}>
                {f}
              </option>
            ))}
          </select>
        </label>
      </div>

      <div className="text-xs text-neutral-600">
        Showing {variants.length} variants (best-effort grouping).
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

To https://github.com/scott48342/warehouse-tire-site.git
   e7f4e9a..b6a07e5  main -> main

## assistant
That makes sense — and I can reproduce *why* it happens on some wheels:

In the TechFeed, the “next size” often **doesn’t keep the same width/offset** (example from your Foose SKU: 18x8 is one SKU, 19x10 is the other). If the selector is comparing strings like `-13` vs `-13.0`, it can fail to find a matching SKU and it “snaps back” to the current one, which feels like the size dropdown won’t change.

### Fix applied
I just pushed a fix that **normalizes numeric fields** (diameter/width/offset) before comparing:

- `18.0` == `18`
- `-13.0` == `-13`
- `10.00` == `10`

and I also fixed the SKU picker to include **bolt pattern** in the “exact match” path.

Deployed to `main`: commit **b6a07e5**.

### What you should do to see it right now
Do a hard refresh / cache-bust on the wheel page:

- Open the wheel and add `?v=1` to the end of the URL (or Ctrl+F5)
  - Example: `/wheels/F22218806140?v=1`

Then try changing Diameter again — it should now navigate to the correct SKU even when width/offset has to change with it.

If it still won’t move, tell me the exact SKU you’re testing and what diameter you’re trying to switch to, and I’ll check the TechFeed style group for that SKU.

## user
why are pictures still not populating
[message_id: b59e452a-4530-4699-a478-f9a61fc1ef61]

## assistant
Most likely because of the condition I used when merging TechFeed → WheelPros:

Right now we only copy TechFeed images **if WheelPros returns no images**:

```ts
if (urls.length && (!Array.isArray(it.images) || it.images.length === 0)) {
  it.images = toWheelProsImages(urls);
}
```

So if WheelPros returns **any** image entry (even a placeholder, broken URL, or something low-quality), we *don’t* overwrite it with the TechFeed `image_url1..4`.

### Quick checks (so I fix the right thing)
1) Is this happening on:
- the **wheel detail page** (`/wheels/<sku>`)  
or
- the **wheel search/listing** page (`/wheels` results cards)?

2) Give me one SKU where images are missing.

### Likely fix
For WheelPros wheels, when TechFeed has images, we should **prefer TechFeed always** (or at least when WheelPros image URLs look “generic/placeholder”).

I can change the merge logic to:

- if `tf.images.length > 0` → set `it.images = techfeedImages` (override)
- otherwise keep WheelPros images

That will guarantee images populate consistently.

If you send me one SKU you’re looking at, I’ll confirm whether TechFeed has image_url(s) for it and then push the override behavior.

## assistant
Because that SKU **isn’t in your TechFeed wheel CSV**, so there’s nothing for us to pull images from.

I checked two ways:

- In the generated TechFeed index: `ABL13-20905215BK` → **not found**
- Directly in the raw CSV file with a text search: **no match** for `ABL13-20905215BK`

And the WheelPros API response for that SKU also shows:

- `"images": []`

So right now we have **no image source at all** for that SKU, which is why the site still shows **“No image”** on `/wheels/ABL13-20905215BK`.

### What we can do next
Pick one:

1) **Confirm the SKU format / mapping**
   - Sometimes the feed uses a different SKU/part number than the API (or a UPC).
   - If you can find that wheel in the CSV by searching “ABL13 20X9 …” or UPC, we can map it.

2) **Add a 2nd fallback image source**
   - If WheelPros has images on a different endpoint (or a predictable CDN path), we can try that when both TechFeed + API images are empty.

3) **Accept “no image” when both sources are empty**
   - But we should at least log/flag these SKUs so you know what’s missing.

If you want, tell me: does WheelPros’ own public site show an image for `ABL13-20905215BK`? If yes, paste that WheelPros URL and I’ll wire up that image source as a fallback.

import Link from "next/link";
import { BRAND } from "@/lib/brand";
import { AutoSubmitSelect } from "@/components/AutoSubmitSelect";

type Wheel = {
  sku?: string;
  brand?: string;
  model?: string;
  finish?: string;
  imageUrl?: string;
  price?: number;
};

type WheelProsBrand = {
  code?: string;
  description?: string;
  parent?: string;
};

type WheelProsImage = {
  imageUrlOriginal?: string;
  imageUrlSmall?: string;
  imageUrlMedium?: string;
  imageUrlLarge?: string;
};

type WheelProsPrice = {
  currencyAmount?: string;
  currencyCode?: string;
};

type WheelProsItem = {
  sku?: string;
  title?: string;
  brand?: WheelProsBrand | string;
  properties?: {
    model?: string;
    finish?: string;
  };
  prices?: {
    msrp?: WheelProsPrice[];
  };
  images?: WheelProsImage[];
};

function getBaseUrl() {
  // On Vercel, prefer the deployment URL.
  if (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL) return process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL;
  if (process.env.VERCEL_URL) return `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}`;
  // Local dev fallback
  return "http://localhost:3000";
}

async function fetchFitment(params: Record<string, string | undefined>) {
  const sp = new URLSearchParams();
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(params)) {
    if (v) sp.set(k, v);
  }

  const res = await fetch(`${getBaseUrl()}/api/vehicles/search?${sp.toString()}`, {
    cache: "no-store",
  });

  if (!res.ok) {
    return { error: await res.text() };
  }

  return res.json();
}

async function fetchWheels(params: Record<string, string | undefined>) {
  const sp = new URLSearchParams();
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(params)) {
    if (v) sp.set(k, v);
  }

  // NOTE: We intentionally call our own API route.
  const res = await fetch(
    `${getBaseUrl()}/api/wheelpros/wheels/search?${sp.toString()}`,
    { cache: "no-store" }
  );

  if (!res.ok) {
    return { error: await res.text() };
  }

  return res.json();
}

export default async function WheelsPage({
  searchParams,
}: {
  searchParams?: Promise<Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>>;
}) {
  const sp = (await searchParams) ?? {};
  const sortRaw = Array.isArray(sp.sort) ? sp.sort[0] : sp.sort;
  const sort = (sortRaw ?? "price_asc").trim();
  const year = (Array.isArray(sp.year) ? sp.year[0] : sp.year) || "";
  const make = (Array.isArray(sp.make) ? sp.make[0] : sp.make) || "";
  const model = (Array.isArray(sp.model) ? sp.model[0] : sp.model) || "";
  const trim = (Array.isArray(sp.trim) ? sp.trim[0] : sp.trim) || "";
  const modification = (Array.isArray(sp.modification) ? sp.modification[0] : sp.modification) || "";

  // Wheel Pros search params are vendor-specific.
  // We’ll start by passing these through; if WP expects different keys,
  // we’ll adapt once we confirm their schema.
  // 1) Resolve fitment (bolt pattern, width/offset ranges, etc.)
  const fitment = year && make && model
    ? await fetchFitment({
        year,
        make,
        model,
        modification: modification || undefined,
      })
    : null;

  const bp: string | undefined = fitment?.boltPattern || undefined;
  // centerbore on WheelPros can be finicky; don't hard-filter on it yet.
  const cb: string | undefined = undefined;

  const diaRange: [number | null, number | null] = Array.isArray(fitment?.wheelDiameterRangeIn)
    ? fitment.wheelDiameterRangeIn
    : [null, null];
  // Width/offset ranges available in fitment, but we are not filtering on them yet.

  // WheelPros expects a single diameter/width.
  // Use the max wheel diameter (more common for OEM packages) and omit width/offset initially,
  // then tighten once we confirm the catalog data lines up.
  const diameterNum = diaRange?.[1] != null ? Number(diaRange[1]) : (diaRange?.[0] != null ? Number(diaRange[0]) : NaN);
  const diameter = Number.isFinite(diameterNum) ? diameterNum.toFixed(1) : undefined;
  const width = undefined;
  const minOffset = undefined;
  const maxOffset = undefined;

  // 2) Query WheelPros using fitment-derived filters
  const data = await fetchWheels({
    page: "1",
    pageSize: "24",
    fields: "inventory,price,images",
    priceType: "msrp",
    // NOTE: WheelPros docs say company is required for pricing, but in practice passing
    // company can zero results for some accounts/environments. Omit for now.
    currencyCode: "USD",

    boltPattern: bp,
    centerbore: cb,
    diameter,
    width,
    minOffset,
    maxOffset,
    offsetType: minOffset || maxOffset ? "RANGE" : undefined,
  });

  const maybeData = data as { items?: unknown[]; results?: unknown[] };

  // common patterns: { items: [] } or { results: [] }
  const rawItems: unknown[] = Array.isArray(maybeData?.items)
    ? maybeData.items
    : (Array.isArray(maybeData?.results) ? maybeData.results : []);

  const itemsUnsorted: Wheel[] = rawItems.map((itUnknown) => {
    const it = itUnknown as WheelProsItem;

    const brandObj = it?.brand && typeof it.brand === "object" ? (it.brand as WheelProsBrand) : null;
    const brand = brandObj?.description ?? brandObj?.parent ?? brandObj?.code ?? (typeof it?.brand === "string" ? it.brand : undefined);
    const finish = it?.properties?.finish;
    const model = it?.properties?.model || it?.title;

    const msrp = it?.prices?.msrp;
    const firstPrice = Array.isArray(msrp) ? msrp[0] : undefined;
    const price = firstPrice?.currencyAmount != null ? Number(firstPrice.currencyAmount) : undefined;

    const img0 = Array.isArray(it?.images) ? it.images[0] : undefined;
    const imageUrl = img0?.imageUrlLarge || img0?.imageUrlMedium || img0?.imageUrlOriginal || undefined;

    return {
      sku: it?.sku,
      brand,
      model,
      finish,
      imageUrl,
      price: typeof price === "number" && Number.isFinite(price) ? price : undefined,
    };
  });

  const items: Wheel[] = [...itemsUnsorted].sort((a, b) => {
    const aPrice = typeof a.price === "number" ? a.price : Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
    const bPrice = typeof b.price === "number" ? b.price : Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;

    switch (sort) {
      case "price_desc":
        return bPrice - aPrice;
      case "brand_asc":
        return String(a.brand || "").localeCompare(String(b.brand || ""));
      case "price_asc":
      default:
        return aPrice - bPrice;
    }
  });

  return (
    <main className="bg-neutral-50">
      <div className="mx-auto max-w-6xl px-4 py-8">
        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-end justify-between gap-3">
          <div>
            <h1 className="text-3xl font-extrabold tracking-tight text-neutral-900">
              Wheels
            </h1>
            <p className="mt-1 text-sm text-neutral-700">
              {year && make && model
                ? `Showing wheels for ${year} ${make} ${model}${trim ? ` ${trim}` : ""}.`
                : "Select your vehicle in the header to filter wheels."}
            </p>
          </div>

          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
            <label className="text-xs font-semibold text-neutral-600">Sort</label>
            <AutoSubmitSelect
              name="sort"
              defaultValue={sort}

[93 more lines in file. Use offset=221 to continue]

## toolResult
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { getTechfeedWheelBySku } from "@/lib/techfeed/wheels";

export const runtime = "nodejs";

type WheelProsSearchResponse = {
  results?: any[];
  [k: string]: any;
};

function toWheelProsImages(urls: string[]) {
  return urls
    .filter(Boolean)
    .map((u) => ({
      imageUrlLarge: u,
      imageUrlMedium: u,
      imageUrlSmall: u,
      imageUrlThumbnail: u,
    }));
}

function hasAnyImage(it: any) {
  const imgs = Array.isArray(it?.images) ? it.images : [];
  return imgs.some((img: any) => {
    const u =
      img?.imageUrlLarge ||
      img?.imageUrlMedium ||
      img?.imageUrlOriginal ||
      img?.imageUrlSmall ||
      img?.imageUrlThumbnail;
    return typeof u === "string" && u.trim().length > 0;
  });
}

export async function GET(req: Request) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const base =
    process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL ||
    process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL;
  if (!base) {
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Missing WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_URL (or NEXT_PUBLIC_WHEELPROS_API_BASE_URL)" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }

  const upstream = new URL("/wheels/search", base);
  url.searchParams.forEach((v, k) => upstream.searchParams.set(k, v));

  const headers: Record<string, string> = { Accept: "application/json" };
  if (process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY) {
    headers["x-api-key"] = process.env.WHEELPROS_WRAPPER_API_KEY;
  }

  const res = await fetch(upstream.toString(), { headers, cache: "no-store" });
  const ct = res.headers.get("content-type") || "application/json";

  let data: WheelProsSearchResponse | null = null;
  try {
    data = (await res.json()) as WheelProsSearchResponse;
  } catch {
    // If upstream didn't return JSON for some reason, just proxy through.
    const text = await res.text();
    return new NextResponse(text, { status: res.status, headers: { "content-type": ct } });
  }

  if (res.ok && data?.results?.length) {
    data.results = await Promise.all(
      data.results.map(async (it) => {
        const sku = it?.sku ? String(it.sku) : "";
        if (!sku) return it;

        const tf = await getTechfeedWheelBySku(sku);
        if (!tf) return it;

        // Ensure properties are populated (WheelPros API sometimes returns blanks).
        it.properties = it.properties || {};
        if (tf.diameter && !it.properties.diameter) it.properties.diameter = tf.diameter;
        if (tf.width && !it.properties.width) it.properties.width = tf.width;
        if (tf.offset && !it.properties.offset) it.properties.offset = tf.offset;
        if (tf.centerbore && !it.properties.centerbore) it.properties.centerbore = tf.centerbore;

        // bolt patterns
        if (tf.bolt_pattern_metric && !it.properties.boltPatternMetric)
          it.properties.boltPatternMetric = tf.bolt_pattern_metric;
        if (tf.bolt_pattern_standard && !it.properties.boltPattern)
          it.properties.boltPattern = tf.bolt_pattern_standard;

        // finish (best effort)
        if (tf.abbreviated_finish_desc && !it.properties.finish)
          it.properties.finish = tf.abbreviated_finish_desc;

        // MSRP: you asked to apply MSRP for WheelPros wheels.
        if (tf.msrp) {
          it.prices = it.prices || {};
          it.prices.msrp = [{ currencyAmount: String(tf.msrp), currencyCode: "USD" }];
        }

        // Images: use techfeed when WheelPros images are missing.
        const urls = tf.images || [];
        if (urls.length && (!Array.isArray(it.images) || it.images.length === 0)) {
          it.images = toWheelProsImages(urls);
        }

        return it;
      })
    );
  }

  return NextResponse.json(data, {
    status: res.status,
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  });
}

## assistant
Yes. I just implemented that.

### What changed
In the WheelPros search API (`/api/wheelpros/wheels/search`), after we enrich results with TechFeed data, we now **filter out any wheel that has no image URL at all** (neither from WheelPros nor TechFeed).

So wheels like `ABL13-20905215BK` (WheelPros `images: []` and not found in TechFeed) will **no longer appear in results**.

### Deployed
Commit: **32a8af7** (pushed to `main`)

### Note
This affects **search/listing results**. If someone navigates directly to a no-image SKU URL, the PDP can still show “No image” (unless you want me to also block/hide those on the detail page too).

## assistant
Yep — that happened because “clicking Wheels” with **no YMM/filters** is basically an unfiltered WheelPros browse call, and a lot of those results come back with no usable image field, so our new “remove no-image wheels” filter wiped the whole list.

I just adjusted it so we **only filter out no-image wheels when the request is actually filtered**, like when you have YMM or you’re searching.

### What changed
In `/api/wheelpros/wheels/search` we now do:

- If query has filters like `sku`, `q`, `brand_cd`, `boltPattern`, `diameter`, etc.  
  → **filter out** items with no images
- If query has none of those (plain browse)  
  → **do not filter**, return everything (so Wheels page won’t be empty)

### Deployed
Commit: **302ce84** (pushed to `main`)

If you refresh `/wheels` now (no vehicle selected) you should see wheels again.

## assistant
Very possible — and with the TechFeed in place it’s actually the *right* way to do wheels.

### What it would look like (Search results page)
Instead of one card per SKU, we show **one card per wheel “style”** (WheelPros family), then underneath the main image we show **small finish thumbnails** (one per finish/color).

Implementation-wise:
- **Group results by `style` (or `display_style_no`)** from TechFeed.
- For each style-group:
  - pick a “default” SKU to represent the card (first in-stock, or cheapest, or first row)
  - collect unique finishes: `abbreviated_finish_desc` / `fancy_finish_desc`
  - for each finish, pick one SKU that has an image and use its image as the thumbnail
- The card links to `/wheels/<defaultSku>`

### What it would look like (Product page)
On the PDP, you’d have a **Finish selector as clickable thumbnails**:
- clicking a finish thumbnail navigates to the SKU for that finish (and optionally preserves the other selections like diameter/width/offset)
- the clicked finish image becomes the **dominant hero image**

We already have the “navigate to SKU on selection” mechanism; we’d just change “Finish” from a `<select>` to a thumbnail strip (or do both).

### Key decision (so we build it the way you want)
When you click a finish thumbnail on the PDP, should it:
1) **Only change finish** (keep diameter/width/bolt/offset locked if possible; if not possible, pick closest match), or
2) Jump to a “canonical” SKU for that finish (even if size changes)?

If you tell me which behavior you prefer, I’ll implement:
- style-grouped wheel cards on `/wheels` results
- finish thumbnails on the card
- finish thumbnail selector on `/wheels/[sku]` that updates the hero image.

## user
it should also change the sku so if it is selected to be installed or ordered then it pushes the correct wheel
[message_id: de938ca6-e191-4be8-a10b-b88c8ddb9586]

## assistant
Perfect — then the rule is:

- **Clicking a finish thumbnail must navigate to the finish’s SKU** (i.e., update the URL to `/wheels/<sku>`), so anything “add to cart / schedule install / order” is tied to the correct SKU.

How I’d implement it (clean + reliable):

### On the product page (PDP)
- Build a **finish → representative SKU** map from the TechFeed style-group (we already load all SKUs in the same `style`).
- Render finish thumbnails (using that SKU’s image URL).
- On click:
  1) find the “best match” SKU with the same current selections (diameter/width/bolt pattern/offset) **plus** the clicked finish, if it exists
  2) otherwise fall back to the clicked finish’s representative SKU
  3) `router.push(/wheels/<thatSku>)`

So it *always* lands on a real SKU page.

### On the wheels search page
- Group by `style`
- Show one card per style
- Show finish thumbnails under the card
- Clicking a finish thumbnail goes directly to that finish’s SKU PDP

### Quick confirmation
Do you want **finish thumbnails to be the primary way to pick finish**, replacing the Finish dropdown — or keep both (dropdown + thumbnails)?

## user
keep both for the time being
